coin_profile.name: Bitcoin
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Store of Value asset is a cryptocurrency whose main purpose is to hold and protect your wealth over time — the same way people have used gold for thousands of years. It does not earn fees from users. Instead, its value comes from scarcity, trust, and the belief that it will be worth more in the future. Bitcoin is the most well-known example. We value these assets by comparing them to gold and looking at the cost of producing new coins.
coin_profile.description: Bitcoin is the original decentralised digital currency and the world's largest cryptocurrency by market cap. It operates on a proof-of-work blockchain with a fixed supply of 21 million coins, making it a programmatically scarce store of value. Often referred to as 'digital gold', Bitcoin is primarily used as a long-term store of value and hedge against inflation.

moat.rationale: BTC's moat is unassailable across all five dimensions: it cannot be meaningfully forked (15+ years of immutable security history and 95%+ supply circulation create network effects that any fork cannot replicate), has absolute dilution control (21M cap), and commands an unmatched hash rate security premium that makes 51% attacks economically irrational. As the original and most decentralized blockchain with zero corporate governance, it has the strongest regulatory resilience, while its store-of-value positioning captures real economic value through monetary premium rather than utility—making it the hardest money ever created and functionally irreplaceable at the settlement layer of crypto.

network_economics.network_synthesis:
• **What This Asset Is:** Bitcoin is digital money designed to work like gold — you hold it as a store of value (a way to save wealth) rather than expecting it to generate income like a business would. It's not evaluated by how much profit it makes, but by how scarce and secure it is.

• **Issuance Rate:** Bitcoin has a fixed issuance rate that automatically shrinks over time through "halving events" (the reward for creating new bitcoins is cut in half roughly every 4 years). This built-in scarcity is the key feature — fewer new bitcoins are created each year, which theoretically makes existing bitcoins more valuable as time goes on.

• **Burn Mechanism:** Bitcoin has no burn mechanism (None%), meaning old bitcoins are never destroyed or removed from circulation. However, this doesn't hurt Bitcoin because the issuance rate is already capped and decreasing — the focus is on a fixed total supply (21 million coins max) rather than burning tokens.

• **Treasury:** Bitcoin has no treasury or central fund because it's decentralized — no company or organization controls it. The network operates through a transparent system where miners are rewarded with newly created bitcoins and transaction fees instead.

• **Verdict:** Bitcoin's supply structure strongly benefits long-term holders because new supply is mathematically limited and shrinking, which means your coins should become relatively scarcer and potentially more valuable over decades.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like digital gold. It does not earn fees — its value comes from people trusting it as a safe place to store wealth, the same way people have trusted gold for centuries. We use two checks: (1) How much of gold's total value has this asset captured so far? If that share grows, the price goes up. (2) How much does it cost to produce one coin? Prices rarely stay below production cost for long because miners would stop mining, reducing supply. The Speculative Fair Value is the average of both checks.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket B (Store of Value): Up to 6 methods are available — Monetary Premium, Production Cost Floor, Metcalfe Network Value, Supply Adjusted Value, Network Activity (NVT), and Rank Benchmark. Monetary Premium measures the token's current share of gold's market cap, projecting fair value if that share is maintained as gold appreciates. Supply Adjusted Value did not fire: FDV/MC is within acceptable range, so no supply dilution penalty was applied. Network Activity (NVT) treats volume as monetary circulation velocity — higher NVT targets apply to SoV assets held long-term rather than traded for fees. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

metric_assessments.scarcity: Bitcoin scores 8 out of 10 on scarcity, which measures how rare and hard-to-produce the asset is—this matters because scarce assets tend to hold value better over time, similar to gold or diamonds. Bitcoin has a supply overhang of only 4.61%, meaning almost all the Bitcoin that will ever exist is already in circulation, making new supply extremely limited. This high scarcity score suggests Bitcoin has strong built-in protection against inflation and devaluation through unlimited printing.
metric_assessments.adoption_momentum: Bitcoin scores 4 out of 10 on adoption momentum, which tracks how widely accepted and used the asset is becoming compared to traditional stores of value like gold. Currently, Bitcoin captures only 7.41% of gold's total market value, indicating that institutional and mainstream adoption is still growing but hasn't reached parity with the 5,000-year-old precious metal. This moderate score means Bitcoin still has significant room to grow if more people and institutions adopt it, but it also suggests adoption growth may be slowing from its earlier explosive phases.
metric_assessments.security_premium: Bitcoin scores 8 out of 10 on security premium, which measures whether the asset's price reflects the cost and difficulty of producing or securing it—this matters because it indicates whether you're paying a reasonable price or an inflated one. Bitcoin's price is 2.22 times its production cost (the energy and hardware required to mine it), which is a healthy multiple showing the market values it significantly above its creation cost without being wildly overpriced. This score suggests Bitcoin's current valuation is backed by real scarcity and security rather than pure speculation.
metric_assessments.monetary_premium_quality: Bitcoin scores 8 out of 10 on monetary premium quality, which evaluates how much of Bitcoin's value comes from it being a reliable monetary asset versus speculative demand. Bitcoin's fully diluted value is only 1.05 times its current market cap, meaning virtually no additional supply will be created in the future, which is ideal for a money-like asset. This near-perfect ratio indicates investors can trust that Bitcoin's value won't be eroded by surprise increases in supply, similar to how gold's value is protected by its natural scarcity.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Bitcoin scores 10 out of 10 on dilution control, which measures how much of the total possible supply is already in circulation—this matters because assets with high circulating percentages won't lose value due to sudden increases in available coins. With 95.39% of all Bitcoin already circulating and new production capped by design, there is virtually no risk of supply surprises or devaluation from future inflation. This perfect score is one of Bitcoin's strongest features for long-term value preservation, especially compared to newer cryptocurrencies that are still in early distribution phases.
metric_assessments.market_resilience: Bitcoin scores 4 out of 10 on market resilience, which measures how stable the price is over short timeframes—this matters because less volatile assets provide more predictable value for planning and saving. Bitcoin's price changed only 2.64% over the past 30 days but showed -0.3% blended volatility over recent weeks, indicating moderate price swings typical of cryptocurrency markets. This moderate score suggests Bitcoin is relatively more stable than many cryptocurrencies but still experiences meaningful price movements that could concern investors seeking rock-solid stability like a savings account.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Bitcoin received a 'Safe' classification due to its exceptional performance in scarcity (8/10), security premium (8/10), monetary premium quality (8/10), and especially dilution control (10/10), which collectively show it has strong fundamental protections against devaluation. While adoption momentum (4/10) and market resilience (4/10) are moderate, the asset's $1.55 trillion market cap and established network security provide stability uncommon in the cryptocurrency space. The overall quality score of 7.5 out of 10 reflects Bitcoin's position as the most trustworthy cryptocurrency for store-of-value purposes.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Bitcoin is a decentralized digital currency and store of value designed to mimic gold—a scarce, globally recognized asset that holds purchasing power over time. Its fundamental strength lies in its absolute scarcity (only 21 million will ever exist), proven security through the world's largest computing network, and massive $1.55 trillion market cap that demonstrates institutional and mainstream acceptance. The main risk is moderate price volatility and the fact that it only captures 7.4% of gold's total value, meaning broader adoption is still uncertain and vulnerable to shifts in investor sentiment. Bitcoin's supply is essentially locked in with 95.39% already circulating and production halving automatically every four years, creating structural protection against inflation that's rare in financial assets. For a beginner seeking a cryptocurrency that behaves like digital gold rather than a speculative bet, Bitcoin's Safe classification and top-ranked position reflect its role as the most established and predictable option in the crypto space, though patience for long-term holding is essential given moderate short-term price swings.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Bitcoin scores 7.5/10 (Safe) as a Store of Value. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket B (Store of Value): Up to 6 methods are available — Monetary Premium, Production Cost Floor, Metcalfe Network Value, Supply Adjusted Value, Network Activity (NVT), and Rank Benchmark. Production Cost Floor estimates the PoW mining cost (×2 fair-value premium) as a fundamental floor — it only fires for mineable assets. Supply Adjusted Value did not fire: FDV/MC is within acceptable range, so no supply dilution penalty was applied. Network Activity (NVT) treats volume as monetary circulation velocity — higher NVT targets apply to SoV assets held long-term rather than traded for fees. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.0/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 26.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
